idioms.title: Bed of roses
idioms.example: Starting a buisness is not a bed of roses

idioms.title: Fresh as daisy
idioms.example: She woke up early and looked fresh as a daisy

idioms.title: Nip in the bud
idioms.example: The teacher nipped the cheating issue in the bud

idioms.title: Every rose has its thorn
idioms.meaning: Every good thing has some problems
idioms.example: I love my job, but every rose has its thorn

idioms.title: Rose-tinted glasses
idioms.meaning: To see everything in an overly positive or unrealistic way
idioms.example: She looks at her past relationship through rose-tinted glasses

idioms.title: Lion's share
idioms.example: She did the lion's share of the work in the project

idioms.title: Let the cat out of the bag
idioms.example: He let the cat out of the bag about the surprise party

idioms.title: Elephant in the room
idioms.example: No one talked about the elephant in the room - the exam results

idioms.title: Hold your horses
idioms.example: Hold your horses!! Let me complete first

idioms.title: Black sheep
idioms.meaning: The odd or different one in a group
idioms.example: She's the black sheep of the family

idioms.title: Black and white
idioms.example: The rules are written in black and white

idioms.title: In the black
idioms.example: The company is finally in the black this year

idioms.title: Black tie event
idioms.example: It's a black-tie dinner tonight

idioms.title: Shopping spree
idioms.meaning: Buying a lot in one go
idioms.example: She went on a shopping spree at the mall

idioms.title: It's a steal
idioms.example: The dress is a steal at that price

idioms.title: Shop till you drop
idioms.meaning: To shop until you are exhausted
idioms.example: We shopped till we dropped during the festival sale

idioms.title: Costs an arm and a leg
idioms.example: The watch costs an arm and a leg

idioms.title: Over the moon
idioms.example: She was over the moon when she got her dream job

idioms.title: Out of the blue
idioms.example: He called me out of the blue after years

idioms.title: Fit as a fiddle
idioms.example: Even at 60, she is fit as a fiddle

idioms.title: Piece of cake
idioms.example: The exam was a piece of cake

idioms.title: Through thick and thin
idioms.example: She stood by me through thick and thin
